steps.ordered: Ultrasound Abdomen (LIVER OR GALLBLADDER US (SINGLE ORGAN))

ensemble.other_hypothesis: choledocholithiasis with biliary obstruction / ascending cholangitis; gallstone pancreatitis with concurrent acute cholecystitis
ensemble.information_gap: The OSH ultrasound demonstrated a 1.9 cm CBD stone and CBD dilation to 8.4 mm with gallbladder wall thickening and pericholecystic fluid, but that study was performed at an outside institution and is not directly available for review; the treating physician needs in-house imaging to confirm CBD stone burden, current CBD diameter, gallbladder status (wall thickness, pericholecystic fluid, hydrops, stones), and to assess for intrahepatic ductal dilation or hepatic parenchymal changes before planning ERCP or surgical intervention.
ensemble.expected_finding: Confirmation of CBD dilation (≥8 mm) with or without a visible obstructing stone, gallbladder wall thickening/pericholecystic fluid consistent with acute cholecystitis, and assessment of intrahepatic bile duct dilation — findings that would guide urgency and route of biliary decompression.
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The OSH ultrasound data are external and unverified at this institution; a repeat in-house RUQ/liver-gallbladder ultrasound is the standard first-line imaging step to confirm biliary anatomy, stone burden, and gallbladder status before proceeding with ERCP or surgical planning in a transferred patient with gallstone pancreatitis and suspected choledocholithiasis.
grounding: History: 'US demonstrated a CBD measuring 8.4mm with a stone in proximal CBD of 1.9cm. The gallbladder has wall thickening with pericholecystic fluid.'
History: 'prior EUS w known CBD dilation (6.7 mm ___)'
History: 'She presented to outside hospital this afternoon' — transferred patient, OSH imaging not directly available
Labs: 'Lipase: 1168.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)' — markedly elevated confirming pancreatitis
Labs: 'Alkaline Phosphatase: 191.0 IU/L', 'AST: 90.0 IU/L', 'ALT: 63.0 IU/L' — obstructive/hepatocellular pattern
Labs: 'Bilirubin, Total: 1.3 mg/dL' — trending down from OSH value of 2.1, but still elevated direct fraction at OSH (Direct: 1.6)
History: 'choluria, subjective fevers and diaphoresis' — raising concern for ascending cholangitis
History: 'Tachycardic to 122' at OSH — hemodynamic instability supporting urgent biliary assessment
ensemble.reasoning: This patient was transferred from an outside hospital with a clinical and biochemical picture of gallstone pancreatitis complicated by choledocholithiasis and possible acute cholecystitis or early cholangitis, but all imaging was performed externally and is unavailable for direct review at this institution. Before committing to ERCP or operative intervention, I need my own verified assessment of the CBD caliber, stone position, gallbladder integrity, and hepatic parenchyma; a bedside RUQ ultrasound is the fastest, safest, and most appropriate first step to confirm the anatomy and guide the urgency of biliary decompression.

representative_ex_ante.appropriateness_reason: The OSH ultrasound data are external and unverified at this institution; a repeat in-house RUQ/liver-gallbladder ultrasound is the standard first-line imaging step to confirm biliary anatomy, stone burden, and gallbladder status before proceeding with ERCP or surgical planning in a transferred patient with gallstone pancreatitis and suspected choledocholithiasis.

verification.actual_finding: The ultrasound demonstrated CBD dilation to 15 mm with two proximal CBD stones (1.5 cm and 1 cm), mild intrahepatic biliary dilation, a markedly abnormal gallbladder with irregular thickened/edematous wall and a focal anterior wall defect communicating with a pericholecystic fluid collection, and a diffusely echogenic liver with a simple hepatic cyst.
verification.relation_to_expectation: The actual findings not only confirmed the expected CBD dilation (≥8 mm) and gallbladder wall thickening/pericholecystic fluid, but exceeded expectations by revealing two CBD stones, more pronounced CBD dilation (15 mm vs. 8.4 mm), intrahepatic ductal dilation, and a gallbladder wall defect with adjacent fluid collection suggesting perforation.

steps.ordered: CT Abdomen (CT ABD & PELVIS WITH CONTRAST)

ensemble.other_hypothesis: Gallbladder perforation with pericholecystic/subhepatic abscess or bile leak; choledocholithiasis with biliary obstruction; complicated cholecystitis (gangrenous or perforated)
ensemble.information_gap: The ultrasound has identified a focal defect in the anterior gallbladder wall connecting to a heterogeneous fluid collection (3.0 x 1.8 cm) along the undersurface of the liver, raising serious concern for gallbladder perforation or bile leak — the extent, nature, and full anatomic characterization of this collection and any associated complications (abscess, free perforation, biliary peritonitis) cannot be determined by ultrasound alone.
ensemble.expected_finding: CT is expected to better delineate the gallbladder wall defect, characterize the pericholecystic/subhepatic fluid collection (simple bile vs. abscess vs. hematoma), assess for free intraperitoneal perforation or biloma, confirm CBD stone burden and degree of biliary dilation, and evaluate pancreatic parenchyma for necrosis or peripancreatic fluid given the markedly elevated lipase.
ensemble.appropriateness_reason: The ultrasound finding of a focal gallbladder wall defect with an adjacent heterogeneous fluid collection is highly concerning for gallbladder perforation, a surgical emergency whose extent and operative implications require cross-sectional imaging with contrast to fully characterize before definitive management decisions are made.
grounding: Ultrasound GALLBLADDER: 'There is a focal defect in the anterior aspect of the wall which connects to a heterogeneous fluid collection medially along the undersurface of the liver. This collection measures approximately 3.0 x 1.8 cm.'
Ultrasound CBD: 'Two stones are seen in the proximal CBD, measuring 1.5 and 1 cm respectively, and the CBD itself appears dilated measuring up to 15 mm.'
History: 'US demonstrated a CBD measuring 8.4mm with a stone in proximal CBD of 1.9cm. The gallbladder has wall thickening with pericholecystic fluid.'
Labs: 'Lipase: 1168.0 IU/L (ref 0.0-60.0)'
Labs: 'White Blood Cells: 13.8 K/uL'; 'Absolute Neutrophil Count: 11.11 K/uL'
History: 'tachycardic to 122'; 'leukocytosis to 16.6k'
Ultrasound GALLBLADDER: 'The gallbladder wall is irregular, thickened and edematous and appears decompressed.'
ensemble.reasoning: The ultrasound has uncovered a potentially catastrophic finding — a focal defect in the gallbladder wall communicating with a heterogeneous subhepatic fluid collection — that raises immediate concern for gallbladder perforation or bile leak superimposed on what was initially framed as gallstone pancreatitis. With a tachycardic, leukocytotic patient who has a markedly dilated CBD with stones, elevated lipase, and now a possible perforated gallbladder, I need contrast-enhanced CT to define the full extent of the collection, confirm or exclude free perforation, assess for biliary peritonitis, and evaluate pancreatic parenchymal integrity before deciding between urgent ERCP, percutaneous drainage, or operative intervention.

verification.actual_finding: CT demonstrated irregular, edematous gallbladder wall with indistinctness inferiorly concerning for perforation, extensive pericholecystic stranding, choledocholithiasis with CBD dilation to 1.9 cm, and normally enhancing pancreatic parenchyma without peripancreatic stranding.
verification.relation_to_expectation: CT fulfilled the expected role by better characterizing the gallbladder wall defect (inferior indistinctness suggesting perforation), confirming choledocholithiasis and biliary dilation, and evaluating the pancreas — though the pericholecystic collection was described as stranding rather than a discrete fluid collection, and no frank abscess or biloma was identified.
